jokes.text: They're keepers of knowledge. She holds the answers to all of our questions, like, 'will you marry me,' and 'why are there still libraries.'
jokes.setup: Romantic idealization of a librarian as 'keeper of knowledge'
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposing marriage proposal with questioning libraries' existence
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection from romantic to practical observation, good economy of language

jokes.text: I wanna be a book. She can pick me up. Flip through my pages. Make sure nobody drew wieners in me.
jokes.setup: Continuing the book metaphor romantically
jokes.punchline: Escalating to juvenile concern about wiener drawings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy childishness, escalation from romantic to juvenile works well

jokes.text: Hey, maybe if we're too loud, she'll shush us.
jokes.setup: Looking for ways to interact with librarian
jokes.punchline: Wanting to be shushed as flirtation strategy
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Abed's analytical approach to romance

jokes.text: Books! Pretend like you're asleep.
jokes.setup: Trying to get librarian's attention by being loud
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting with fake sleeping
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy bit, gets the job done but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, I don't believe in dibs or love at first sight or love or best friends or doing things.
jokes.setup: Troy asking for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical escalation ending with 'doing things'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff cynicism with great escalating structure, highly quotable

jokes.text: You're gonna have to open your heart one day, Jeff. What happens if I don't? I miss the heart opening deadline?
jokes.setup: Troy's earnest advice about opening his heart
jokes.punchline: Jeff treating it like a bureaucratic deadline
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - Jeff deflecting emotion with procedural humor

jokes.text: Valentine's Crapentine's. Opening my heart is on my list.
jokes.setup: Continuing the heart opening conversation
jokes.punchline: Juvenile wordplay followed by treating emotion as a to-do item
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay but character-appropriate cynicism saves it somewhat

jokes.text: Pierce? What was that? Gum. Why aren't you chewing? Because I'm using my mouth for this dumb conversation.
jokes.setup: Annie noticing Pierce's suspicious mouth activity
jokes.punchline: Pierce's hostile deflection while clearly lying
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Pierce's drug subplot, his cranky deflection feels authentic

jokes.text: Before you go barking up that tree, I have to tell you that she might not be interested. Why wouldn't she be... Oh! Is she... A friend of Ellen?
jokes.setup: Britta warning Troy about Page's potential disinterest
jokes.punchline: Troy's euphemistic confusion about sexuality
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent confusion works well for his character, setup for larger misunderstanding

jokes.text: Yes, Annie. But you can say the word. Page is a...Lesbian.
jokes.setup: Troy's euphemistic approach
jokes.punchline: Britta's overly dramatic pause before saying 'lesbian'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central misunderstanding, Britta's false confidence is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Pierce, as someone who's been calling me a lesbian for the last year and a half, I'm sure that you don't have anything to say about me being friends with one. Nothing off the cuff. What I do have is a prepared statement.
jokes.setup: Britta expecting Pierce to make inappropriate comments
jokes.punchline: Pierce having literally prepared a statement about this scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Pierce being both offensive and oddly prepared is perfect

jokes.text: And in summation, good luck... And bon appetit.
jokes.setup: Pierce reading his prepared statement about Britta and lesbians
jokes.punchline: Ending with completely inappropriate 'bon appetit'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Pierce moment, 'bon appetit' is hilariously wrong context

jokes.text: Many, many paragraphs of that were oddly supportive. Wait till you hear the one I have for you.
jokes.setup: Pierce's prepared statement being unexpectedly supportive
jokes.punchline: Pierce having another prepared statement for someone else
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Pierce's over-preparedness, maintains character consistency

jokes.text: Okay. I know that wasn't gum. You're right. It was an awesome exit pill. Zip! Zop! Zooey! Bye!
jokes.setup: Annie confronting Pierce about the 'gum'
jokes.punchline: Pierce's nonsensical sound effects while dramatically exiting on drugs
jokes.explanation: Pierce's drugged behavior is appropriately ridiculous, sound effects add to the absurdity

jokes.text: He's recovering dude can moonwalk! Besides, he's a baby boom. They invented drugs. Yeah, they also invented TV. Have you seen him control one of those?
jokes.setup: Troy defending Pierce's drug use by citing baby boomers inventing drugs
jokes.punchline: Annie's counterpoint about Pierce's inability with modern technology
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic reversal, great observational comedy about generational competence

jokes.text: Well, he also listens to the Barenaked Ladies. Go get their dumb asses to help you.
jokes.setup: Group saying Pierce listens to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive comment about Barenaked Ladies
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Barenaked Ladies running gag, Jeff's snobbery is in character

jokes.text: But Pierce is our friend, and the Barenaked Ladies are triple platinum... Are you? Why does everyone leap to defend that band so aggressively?
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing Barenaked Ladies
jokes.punchline: Annie's passionate defense and Jeff's bewildered response
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the BNL fight, Annie's unexpected passion is funny

jokes.text: And maybe BNL has two Billboard awards to your zero. Oh, okay. They're... they're BNL now. We use shorthand for the Barenaked Ladies. That's how fundamental they are.
jokes.setup: The Barenaked Ladies argument escalating
jokes.punchline: Jeff's incredulous reaction to the band having an acronym
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurd fandom, Jeff's disbelief is perfectly played, highly quotable

jokes.text: Maybe we all need some space to pull the knife out of the back of the most celebrated Canadian alt rock band of the mid-nineties, you selfish, jaded ass!
jokes.setup: The argument reaching peak intensity
jokes.punchline: Annie's over-the-top defense with oddly specific description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie passion escalation, the specific genre description makes it funnier

jokes.text: This...is a fight! We...are fighting!
jokes.setup: The heated argument reaching its climax
jokes.punchline: Annie explicitly announcing what's happening
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment, Annie's earnest announcement of the obvious is very her

jokes.text: I am a stylish American, professor. I've been forcing myself to be into soccer since 2004.
jokes.setup: Duncan assuming Jeff doesn't know soccer
jokes.punchline: Jeff's admission of performative soccer fandom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff pretension, the forced enthusiasm rings very true to his character

jokes.text: You promised the study group? Whip, crack! I'll see you at precisely 6:30, or as the English call it, gravedigger's biscuits.
jokes.setup: Jeff mentioning his promise to the study group
jokes.punchline: Duncan's made-up British time terminology
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd fake British expression, fits Duncan's character well

jokes.text: I'll see you at precisely 6:30, or as the English call it, gravedigger's biscuits.
jokes.setup: Duncan setting a meeting time
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical British time terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - completely made-up British terminology that sounds plausible. Great character bit for Duncan.

jokes.text: That moonwalk back there was streets ahead, buddy. Hey, thanks, buddy. Hey, Pierce! Hey, it's you! Yeah. The tiny man you see when you take enough pills.
jokes.setup: Chang complimenting Pierce's moonwalking
jokes.punchline: Pierce's drugged perception of Chang as a hallucination
jokes.explanation: Good drug humor, Pierce's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: Pierce, if doctors are so smart, why are there millions of 'em?
jokes.setup: Pierce worrying about doctor approval for more pills
jokes.punchline: Chang's nonsensical logic about doctor intelligence
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang illogic, the faulty reasoning is appropriately stupid

jokes.text: Are you the guys that keep staring at me and intermittently yelling out, 'books'?
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed introducing themselves to Mariah
jokes.punchline: Her calling out their weird library behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their earlier library antics, appropriately embarrassing

jokes.text: It is of utmost importance we protect that friendship from the stresses of courting you, so we're trying to be as direct and aboveboard about this as possible.
jokes.setup: Explaining their romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Abed's overly formal, analytical approach to romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed character work - treating romance like a business negotiation

jokes.text: What's in the briefcase? Oh. Tacos. You want one? No. Great. We really wanted them.
jokes.setup: Mariah noticing their mysterious briefcase
jokes.punchline: Mundane contents and their relief at not sharing
jokes.explanation: Good visual subversion and honest character moment about food priorities

jokes.text: So like...Would you change clothes in front of her? If you have to ask if it's homophobic to ask questions, haven't you already answered your own question? Have I? Don't know. Not a homophobe.
jokes.setup: Annie's innocent curiosity about lesbian relationships
jokes.punchline: Britta's convoluted logic creating more confusion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about well-meaning ignorance and pseudo-intellectual responses

jokes.text: Hey. Hi. I'm not a lesbian. Oh! I'm not either. Oh. But it's cool that you're friends with one. Oh.
jokes.setup: Page and Annie meeting with mutual misconceptions
jokes.punchline: The cascade of 'Oh!' realizations
jokes.explanation: Well-structured confusion comedy, the rhythm of realizations works well

jokes.text: Page isn't a lesbian. She just likes hanging out with Britta, because it makes her feel cool to have a lesbian friend. But...Britta's not a lesbian. She thinks that Page is.
jokes.setup: The lesbian misunderstanding being revealed
jokes.punchline: The full circular irony of mutual misconceptions
jokes.explanation: Excellent plotting revelation - perfectly circular irony that drives the episode's conflict

jokes.text: Hope I stay straight, right? Oh, sorry. My friend's a tad homophobic, so I was doing a bit.
jokes.setup: Britta performing her lesbian identity
jokes.punchline: Casually throwing Annie under the bus
jokes.explanation: Good Britta performative wokeness, casually sacrificing Annie is in character

jokes.text: Pretty exciting first half. Yeah, 0-0. Electrifying.
jokes.setup: Duncan's enthusiasm for the soccer match
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic response to the scoreless game
jokes.explanation: Standard sports boredom joke, gets the job done but not particularly creative

jokes.text: That's better, you big slut.
jokes.setup: Jeff turning off his phone
jokes.punchline: Calling his phone a slut
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing personification of technology, fits Jeff's character

jokes.text: Don't mind if I Chang! Heh heh. Hey, man, check this out. Ya-haa! Hoo hoo hoo hoo hoo!
jokes.setup: Chang entering Jeff's apartment
jokes.punchline: Name pun followed by nonsensical celebration sounds
jokes.explanation: Typical Chang pun and energy, predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Hey. In England, fanny means vagina, right? In England, everything means vagina.
jokes.setup: Chang asking about British terminology
jokes.punchline: Duncan's exaggerated response about British language
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from specific to absurd, plays on British slang stereotypes well

jokes.text: In england we call them Italian fannies.
jokes.setup: Chang wanting to order pizza
jokes.punchline: Duncan's fake British term following his 'everything means vagina' rule
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the absurd logic, Duncan committing to the bit

jokes.text: Cellie... Ah. Go use the landline. Landline? Okay, grandma Bell.
jokes.setup: Jeff telling Chang to use the landline
jokes.punchline: Chang's outdated reference to Alexander Graham Bell
jokes.explanation: Decent technology generation gap humor, Chang's confusion is believable

jokes.text: So Mariah, what you get with me is the complete package... HBO, Showtime, Starz, Starz black.
jokes.setup: Troy pitching himself romantically as 'complete package'
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's talking about cable TV channels
jokes.explanation: Great Troy innocence - thinking cable packages are impressive romantic offerings

jokes.text: Let's do it. Okay.
jokes.setup: Deciding to give people something to 'really talk about'
jokes.punchline: Both committing to fake lesbian performance
jokes.explanation: Setup for visual gag, both characters committing to false identities for social validation

jokes.text: Oh, nice call, ref! Bite my banger!
jokes.setup: Jeff watching soccer and getting into it
jokes.punchline: Using British slang incorrectly in anger
jokes.explanation: Jeff's pretentious cultural performance backfiring, appropriate misuse of slang

jokes.text: Leonard? Where are the white women at? No. There are no white women here, Leonard. This is not a party!
jokes.setup: Leonard arriving at what he thinks is a party
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate Blazing Saddles reference and Jeff's panic
jokes.explanation: Good Leonard character moment with movie reference, Jeff's desperation is funny

jokes.text: Yo, yo, yo, yo, yo! Pop, pop! Bam!
jokes.setup: Magnitude's arrival at the 'party'
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Magnitude introduction - one-note character executed flawlessly

jokes.text: That's my analysis of the Saw movie franchise.
jokes.setup: Abed finishing what was clearly a very long monologue
jokes.punchline: Revealing he spent hours analyzing Saw movies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed character moment - his obsessive film analysis as romantic conversation

jokes.text: No. I choose you, Troy. Punch. Hey, Abed. Hey. I, um... She... Just... She chose me. Cool. You can have this then.
jokes.setup: Mariah choosing Troy over Abed
jokes.punchline: Abed's understated reaction and casual punch handoff
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Abed's emotional detachment and practical response to rejection

jokes.text: Pierce? You go straight to hell! Wh... Ha... Oh, I'm sorry, Annie. I...I've been... I don't know. I've been going through all these mood swings lately.
jokes.setup: Pierce's seemingly random outburst at Annie
jokes.punchline: His immediate awareness and apology for drug-induced behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce drug subplot development, his self-awareness makes it more than just random outburst

jokes.text: Ha ha! Look at the lesbians. Lesbians! Screw you! Screw you!
jokes.setup: Britta and Page's fake lesbian performance
jokes.punchline: Getting genuine harassment that breaks their act
jokes.explanation: Good consequence for their performance, reality check that breaks their comfortable play-acting

jokes.text: Well, I mean, he did talk about the Saw movies for two hours. Yeah, but there are eight of them, and they're two hours apiece, and the first one did redefine the genre, and... Troy, I would love to be his friend.
jokes.setup: Mariah explaining why she didn't choose Abed
jokes.punchline: Troy immediately defending Abed's Saw obsession in detail
jokes.explanation: Beautiful friendship moment - Troy instinctively defending Abed even while on a date

jokes.text: I hate her, I hate her, I hate her, I hate her! What happened? She called you weird! I am weird. Not as weird as her! Who the hell is Nicholas Nickleby?
jokes.setup: Abed being upset about his date
jokes.punchline: Troy's immediate defensive anger and confusion about literature
jokes.explanation: Great friendship chemistry - Troy's protective instincts and cultural confusion are perfect

jokes.text: It's weird, but I've never heard of Magnitude before tonight. He's a one-man party! Oh, yeah! Pop, pop!
jokes.setup: Jeff not knowing who Magnitude is
jokes.punchline: Magnitude immediately proving Duncan's point with his signature enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction and immediate demonstration of Magnitude's one-note personality

jokes.text: I never said I was a lesbian. Why didn't you just ask me? Oh, what am I, a homophobe? I don't care about people's preferences. You so care.
jokes.setup: Page pointing out the communication failure
jokes.punchline: Britta's defensive response revealing she does care about seeming progressive
jokes.explanation: Good Britta character moment - her performative wokeness getting called out directly

jokes.text: You're the worst! What? And for the record, I never thought you were cool. I only thought you were a lesbian.
jokes.setup: The argument escalating between Page and Britta
jokes.punchline: Page's brutal honesty about their mutual superficiality
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and revelation - both characters admitting their superficial motivations

jokes.text: Are you wearing my underwear? Oh. I just needed a pair while I did some laundry here. I don't have a washing machine.
jokes.setup: Jeff discovering Chang's increasing intrusion
jokes.punchline: Chang casually admitting to using Jeff's underwear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chang's boundary violations, appropriately uncomfortable

jokes.text: I'm homeless, Jeff, okay? When my wife found out about Shirley and me, she kicked me out... For good this time! My whole life is in this dishwasher, man! Well, some of it's also soaking in your bathtub.
jokes.setup: Chang's increasing intrusion being revealed as homelessness
jokes.punchline: The pathetic image of his belongings in kitchen appliances
jokes.explanation: Good character development revelation with appropriately pathetic visual details

jokes.text: Hey, Jeff, don't kill the messenger, but you have four hermit crabs in your bathroom sink. You'll fall in love with them. I named one Jeff.
jokes.setup: Chang's continuing invasion of Jeff's space
jokes.punchline: Introducing pets and naming one after Jeff
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Chang's presumptuous behavior, naming the crab Jeff is perfect touch

jokes.text: Star-burns, Leonard, Magnitude. Pop, pop swizzle, scandalous, C Dubb, Tim. Mighty D. Glisten.
jokes.setup: Jeff kicking everyone out
jokes.punchline: The absurd list of background character names
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor with ridiculous background character names

jokes.text: What is it about me that makes broken people flock to me? Is it my height? Do huddled masses mistake me for the Statue of Liberty?
jokes.setup: Jeff reflecting on attracting troubled people
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to the Statue of Liberty through height
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff self-reflection with great visual metaphor connecting his height to symbolic welcoming

jokes.text: Let's turn on my phone and listen to the 39 messages they left tonight. You have no new messages.
jokes.setup: Jeff expecting to prove his friends' neediness
jokes.punchline: No messages at all, subverting his expectations
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that reveals Jeff's actual need for the group, great structure

jokes.text: Caring about a person can be scary. Caring about six people can be a horrifying embarrassing nightmare. At least for me.
jokes.setup: Jeff's emotional growth speech
jokes.punchline: Escalating from scary to nightmare, then undercutting with 'at least for me'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff emotional honesty with characteristic self-deprecation and escalation

jokes.text: You're cute? No, wait. Kiss me. There has got to be a way to make money off of this!
jokes.setup: Pierce watching Troy and Abed's reconciliation
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate business instinct about their friendship
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce inappropriate response - turning heartfelt moment into money-making opportunity

jokes.text: I've been cheating. No. It says email me.
jokes.setup: Pierce's apparent confession of cheating
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's just reading a Valentine's card wrong
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about Pierce's reading comprehension, appropriately awkward ending
